title: useUndoRedo Hook
description:
Implement a **useUndoRedo** custom hook that provides full undo/redo history management for any state value.

### Requirements

- `useUndoRedo<T>(initialValue: T)` returns an object with:
  - `state` — the current value
  - `set(value: T)` — push a new value (clears any future redo stack)
  - `undo()` — revert to the previous value
  - `redo()` — re-apply a previously undone value
  - `canUndo` — boolean, true when there is history to undo
  - `canRedo` — boolean, true when there is history to redo
  - `reset(value?: T)` — clear all history and set state to `value` (or `initialValue` if omitted)
  - `history` — object `{ past: T[], present: T, future: T[] }` exposing the full stack
- Setting a new value clears the redo (future) stack
- Calling undo when `canUndo` is false should be a no-op
- Calling redo when `canRedo` is false should be a no-op

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Current state display | `current` |
| Undo button | `undo` |
| Redo button | `redo` |
| Reset button | `reset` |
| History length display | `history-length` |
constraints:
- Implement in useUndoRedo.ts as a standalone hook
- Use useReducer or useState internally
- set(), undo(), redo(), reset() must all be stable references (wrap in useCallback or derive from dispatch)
- Must not mutate arrays — use immutable operations
hints: Model the state as { past: T[], present: T, future: T[] } and use useReducer with action types SET, UNDO, REDO, RESET.
For SET: push present onto past, set new present, clear future.
For UNDO: pop from past → new present, push old present onto future.
For REDO: pop from future → new present, push old present onto past.
